title: Symphyotrichum anticostense
intro: Symphyotrichum anticostense, also known as Anticosti aster, is a rare species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae), native to the Anticosti Island in Quebec, Canada. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to act as a natural antiseptic and antimicrobial agent. The bioactive constituents of Symphyotrichum anticostense include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Symphyotrichum anticostense belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it within the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. It is also a member of the genus Symphyotrichum and the species S. anticostense. The classification of S. anticostense is further supported by its shared characteristics with other members of the Asteraceae family, such as its composite flower structure. The classification of S. anticostense is a reflection of its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the same family.
